title: rubus hedycarpus var. vulnificus
intro: Rubus hedycarpus var. vulnificus, also known as the Western poison oak or poison oak, is a variety of the Eastern poison oak (Rhus radicans) plant. The Western poison oak contains medicinal properties, including flavonoids and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, acting as an antiseptic, and exhibiting anti-cancer properties. The Western poison oak is typically prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use requires caution due to its potential allergenic and toxic effects.
